102	/-! ## §1. The line bundle of a closed point on a smooth proper curve
103	
104	For a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` and a closed
105	point `P ∈ C`, the local ring `𝒪_{C,P}` is a DVR with maximal ideal
106	generated by a uniformiser `f_P`. Hartshorne's construction `ℒ(D)`
107	(II §6 p. 144) applied to the Cartier divisor `[P]` (locally cut out by
108	`f_P` near `P`, by `1` elsewhere) produces the invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(P)`:
109	the sub-`𝒪_C`-module of the function-field constant sheaf `𝒦_C ≅ K(C)`
110	generated locally by `f_P^{-1}` near `P` and by `1` on `C \ {P}`. -/

116	
117	The invertible sheaf cut out (in the dual / `𝒦_C`-subsheaf packaging of
118	Hartshorne `ℒ(D)`) by `f_P^{-1}` near `P` and by `1` on the complement,
119	where `f_P ∈ 𝔪_P ∖ 𝔪_P²` is any uniformiser of the DVR `𝒪_{C,P}`. The
120	result is independent of the choice of uniformiser (two uniformisers
121	differ by a unit) and is an invertible `𝒪_C`-module of rank one.
122

126	that `H⁰` and `H¹` of `𝒪_C(P)` are accessible via
127	`Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP) 0/1`).

159	/-! ## §2. Global sections as the Riemann–Roch space `L([P])`
160	
161	Hartshorne II §7 Proposition 7.7 identifies the global sections of
162	`ℒ(D_0)` with the rational functions `f ∈ K(X)^×` satisfying
163	`div(f) ≥ −D_0` (plus the zero section). Specialised to `D_0 = [P]` on a
164	curve, the condition `div(f) + [P] ≥ 0` rewrites coordinate-wise as
165	`ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`. -/
166	
167	/-- **Global sections of `𝒪_C(P)` as rational functions with controlled
168	pole at `P`** (Hartshorne II §7 Proposition 7.7, p. 157).
169	
170	For a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(C)^×`, the following are
171	equivalent:
172	
173	* `f` defines a global section of `𝒪_C(P)` (equivalently: there exists
174	  a nonzero `s ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` whose image under the canonical
175	  inclusion `𝒪_C(P) ↪ 𝒦_C ≅ K(C)` is `f`);
176	* the order conditions hold: `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor
177	  `Q ∈ C.PrimeDivisor` whose generic point is not `P`, and
178	  `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` (where the latter is read off the prime divisor
179	  `⟨P, h⟩` with `h : Order.coheight P = 1` the codimension-one witness
180	  automatic for a closed point on a one-dimensional integral scheme).
181	
182	The iff is the substantive content of Hartshorne's Proposition 7.7(b) /
183	its proof, specialised to `D_0 = [P]`.
184	
185	iter-177+ body: unfold `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` as the subsheaf of
186	`𝒦_C` generated locally by `f_P⁻¹` near `P` and by `1` elsewhere, then
187	read off the order conditions at each stalk via the DVR valuation
188	identification.
189	
190	Blueprint reference: `lem:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_globalSections_iff`
191	(Hartshorne II.7 Proposition 7.7(b), p. 157). -/

193	    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
194	        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
195	    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
196	    (f : C.left.functionField) (_hf : f ≠ 0)
197	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1) :
198	    (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ≠ P →
199	        0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
200	      (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ f
201	    ↔
202	    Nonempty
203	      { s : Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP) 0 //
204	        s ≠ 0 } := by
205	  -- iter-180 Lane D structural advance: split the Iff into both
206	  -- directions. Each direction needs a concrete body for
207	  -- `lineBundleAtClosedPoint` (which itself is the iter-180 typed `sorry`

211	  -- the backward direction is the "section → divisor of zeros" of 7.7(a).
212	  refine ⟨fun _h => ?_, fun _h => ?_⟩
213	  · -- Forward (Hartshorne II.7.7(b)): given the order conditions on `f`,
214	    -- the rational function `f` defines a global section of `𝒪_C(P)`
215	    -- (the section corresponds to multiplication by `f` on the

218	    sorry
219	  · -- Backward (Hartshorne II.7.7(a)): given a non-zero global section
220	    -- `s` of `𝒪_C(P)`, read off the order conditions stalk-by-stalk via
221	    -- the DVR identification `𝒪_C(P)_Q = 𝒪_{C,Q}` for `Q ≠ P` and
222	    -- `𝒪_C(P)_P = f_P^{-1} · 𝒪_{C,P}`.  Requires the same body of

226	/-! ## §3. Cohomological vanishing in genus zero
227	
228	Specialise Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3's inductive step at `D = 0`. The
229	standard short exact sequence
230	`0 → 𝒪_C(−[P]) → 𝒪_C → k(P) → 0` (Hartshorne II.6.18: the ideal sheaf
231	of the locally principal closed subscheme `P` is `𝒪_C(−[P])`; the
232	quotient is the skyscraper `k(P) ≅ k̄` at `P`) tensored by the locally
233	free rank-`1` sheaf `𝒪_C([P])` (left rigid, so preserves exactness and
234	leaves the skyscraper invariant) becomes
235	`0 → 𝒪_C → 𝒪_C(P) → k(P) → 0` in `Coh(C)`. The associated long exact
236	sequence of sheaf cohomology, combined with `H¹(C, 𝒪_C) = 0`
237	(genus-`0` hypothesis: `g(C) = dim_{k̄} H¹(C, 𝒪_C)`) and
238	`H¹(C, k(P)) = 0` (skyscraper / flasque), kills `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P))`. -/
239	
240	/-- **Vanishing of `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P))` on a smooth proper geometrically
241	irreducible curve of genus `0`** (Hartshorne IV §1 p. 296, the
242	inductive step of Theorem 1.3 specialised to `D = 0`).
243	
244	Concretely, the finite-dimensional `k̄`-vector space
245	`Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint P hP) 1` has dimension
246	`0`, i.e. is the trivial vector space.
247	
248	iter-177+ body: assemble the closed-point short exact sequence
249	`0 → 𝒪_C → 𝒪_C(P) → k(P) → 0`, feed it to the long exact sequence of
250	`Module k̄`-flavoured cohomology (the project's
251	`Scheme.HModule k̄`-bridge inherits the LES by forget-functor
252	naturality from
253	`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariantSequence_exact`), substitute
254	`H¹(C, 𝒪_C) = 0` (the genus-`0` hypothesis, unfolding
255	`AlgebraicGeometry.genus`) and `H¹(C, k(P)) = 0` (skyscraper sheaf /
256	flasque cohomology, Hartshorne III.2.5), and collapse the segment to
257	`0 → H¹(C, 𝒪_C(P)) → 0`.
258	
259	Blueprint reference: `lem:H1_vanishing_lineBundleAtClosedPoint_genusZero`
260	(Hartshorne IV.1 p. 296). -/
261	lemma h1_vanishing_genusZero
262	    (P : C.left) (hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
263	    (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0) :
264	    Module.finrank kbar
265	        (Scheme.HModule kbar (lineBundleAtClosedPoint (C := C) P hP) 1) = 0 := by
266	  sorry

268	/-! ## §4. The dimension formula `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2` in genus zero
269	
270	Specialise the Euler-characteristic identity
271	`χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` of `RR.2`
272	(`Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus`) to
273	`D = [P]`. Since `deg([P]) = 1` (every closed point contributes degree
274	`1` over `k̄`) and `g(C) = 0`, this gives `χ(𝒪_C(P)) = 2`. Unfolding
275	`χ` as `dim H⁰ − dim H¹` and substituting the `H¹`-vanishing of §3
276	yields `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`. -/
277	
278	/-- **The dimension formula `dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2` on a smooth
279	proper geometrically irreducible genus-`0` curve over `k̄`**

303	/-! ## §5. A non-constant rational function with at most a simple pole at `P`
304	
305	The two-dimensionality of `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))` and the one-dimensional
306	constant subspace `k̄ · 1` give a non-zero quotient `H⁰/k̄`. Any lift
307	of a non-zero element of the quotient is, under the identification of
308	`globalSections_iff`, a non-constant rational function `f ∈ K(C)` with
309	the order conditions `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for `Q ≠ P` and `ord_P(f) ≥ −1`.
310	This is the seed of `RR.4` (the morphism `C → ℙ¹` produced by
311	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` from the basis `(1, f)`). -/
312	
313	/-- **Existence of a non-constant rational function regular on `C ∖ {P}`
314	with at most a simple pole at `P`** (Hartshorne IV §1 Exercise 1.1,
315	p. 297, the genus-`0` specialisation).
316	
317	Concretely, there exists `f ∈ K(C)` such that:
318	
319	* `f ≠ 0`;
320	* `f ∉ k̄` (i.e. `f` is non-constant — for instance, it does not lie
321	  in the image of the structural inclusion of constants);
322	* `ord_Q(f) ≥ 0` for every prime divisor `Q ∈ C.PrimeDivisor` whose
323	  generic point is not `P`;
324	* `ord_P(f) ≥ −1` (at most a simple pole at `P`).
325	
326	iter-177+ body: use `dim_eq_two_of_genusZero` to get
327	`dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P)) = 2`. The image of `1 ∈ H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) ≅ k̄`
328	under the structural inclusion `𝒪_C ↪ 𝒪_C(P)` spans a one-dimensional
329	subspace of `H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(P))`; choose any element of the complement
330	(non-empty because `dim H⁰ = 2 > 1`), lift it through
331	`globalSections_iff` to a rational function `f`, and verify that the
332	three bullets hold. The lift is non-constant because it is not in the
333	constant subspace.
334	
335	The principal-divisor-non-zero formulation `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal
336	f hf ≠ 0` follows from non-constancy plus the fact that constant
337	functions have principal divisor zero (the converse — `div(f) = 0`
338	⇒ `f` constant — uses the integrality of `C` and is the Stacks 02P0
339	"functions with no zeros and poles are constant" type statement).
340	
341	Blueprint reference:
342	`cor:lineBundleAtClosedPoint_exists_nonconstant_genusZero` (alias
343	`cor:nonconstant_function_genus_zero` consumed by `RR.4`)
344	(Hartshorne IV.1 Exercise 1.1, p. 297). -/
345	theorem exists_nonconstant_genusZero
346	    [∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor,
347	        Ring.KrullDimLE 1 (C.left.presheaf.stalk Q.point)]
348	    (P : C.left) (_hP : IsClosed ({P} : Set C.left))
349	    (hPcoh : Order.coheight P = 1)
350	    (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0) :
351	    ∃ (f : C.left.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0),
352	      (∀ Q : C.left.PrimeDivisor, Q.point ≠ P →
353	          0 ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order Q f) ∧
354	      (-1 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.RationalMap.order ⟨P, hPcoh⟩ f ∧
355	      Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal (X := C.left) f hf ≠ 0 := by
356	  sorry

10	/-!
11	# The Riemann–Roch formula in genus zero (RR.2)
12	
13	This file is the **RR.2** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
14	headline `genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geometrically
15	irreducible genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in
16	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
17	
18	The Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain for the genus-`0` ↦ `ℙ¹` classification routes
19	through:
20	
21	- `RR.1` (`RiemannRoch_WeilDivisor.tex` / `WeilDivisor.lean`): the Weil
22	  divisor group `Div(C)` and the degree map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ`.
23	- **`RR.2` (this file, `RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex`)**: the Riemann–Roch
24	  dimension formula `ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1` in genus `0` (with `deg D ≥ 0`),
25	  via the auxiliary Euler-characteristic identity
26	  `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g`.
27	- `RR.3` (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, future): the invertible sheaf
28	  `𝒪_C(D)`, the linear-equivalence isomorphism `𝒪_C(D) ≅ 𝒪_C(D')` for
29	  `D ∼ D'`, and the `H¹`-vanishing input
30	  `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(D)) = 0` for `deg D ≥ 0` on a genus-`0` curve.
31	- `RR.4` (`RiemannRoch_RationalIsoP1.tex`, future): the "two-section
32	  ⇒ `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` ⇒ `≅ ℙ¹`" classification.
33	
34	## Status (iter-174 Lane F file-skeleton)
35	
36	This file is the **iter-174 Lane F** file-skeleton: each of the four pinned
37	declarations carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
38	blueprint `\lean{...}` pin in `chapters/RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex`). The
39	Euler-characteristic carrier definition is concrete (a one-line subtraction
40	of `H⁰` and `H¹` `Module.finrank`s, mirroring the `genus` definition of
41	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus`); the remaining pins carry `sorry` bodies whose
42	closure is iter-175+ work after the sibling chapters `RR.3`
43	(`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`) and `RR.4` (`RiemannRoch_RationalIsoP1.tex`) land.
44	
45	The 4 pinned declarations are:
46	
47	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic` — Euler characteristic
48	   `χ(𝓕) = dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝓕) − dim_{k̄} H¹(C, 𝓕)` of a `ModuleCat k̄`-valued
49	   sheaf on `C` (the curve specialisation of the alternating sum, since
50	   `H^i = 0` for `i ≥ 2` on a one-dimensional scheme by Grothendieck
51	   vanishing).
52	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.l` — the `ℓ`-invariant
53	   `ℓ(D) = dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(D))` of a Weil divisor `D`.
54	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus`
55	   — the Euler-characteristic identity `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g` for
56	   every `D ∈ Div(C)` on a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
57	   `C / k̄` of genus `g = g(C)`.
58	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero`
59	   — the Riemann–Roch formula in genus `0`: `ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1` for any
60	   Weil divisor `D ∈ Div(C)` with `deg D ≥ 0` on a smooth proper
61	   geometrically irreducible curve `C / k̄` with `g(C) = 0` (threading
62	   the `H¹`-vanishing of `𝒪_C(D)` explicitly as a named premise until
63	   `RR.3` lands).
64	
65	## Note on `𝒪_C(D)` (the invertible sheaf of a divisor)
66	
67	The chapter's proof of `eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus`
68	and the statement of `l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero` both reference
69	the line bundle `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D`. Mathlib `b80f227` ships no
70	`Scheme.lineBundleOfDivisor` (the closest is `WeierstrassCurve.lineBundle`
71	in the elliptic-curve formalisation), and the project-side construction of
72	`𝒪_C(D)` is queued for `RR.3` (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`). To keep the type
73	signatures of pins 2–4 substantive in the iter-174 skeleton, we expose a
74	**typed-`sorry` placeholder**
75	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf` that pairs each divisor with
76	the `ModuleCat k̄`-valued sheaf carrier `𝒪_C(D)` is intended to occupy. The
77	iter-175+ closure of `RR.3` replaces this placeholder's body with the
78	honest invertible-sheaf construction; the present pins consume it only
79	through its `H⁰` and `H¹` cohomology, so the consumer signatures are
80	substantive in the type sense (each asserts an arithmetic identity on the
81	finiteness-of-`H^*` outputs).
82	
83	## References
84	
85	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex` (Hartshorne
86	IV.1 verbatim quotes; 4 pins).
87	Source: Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, IV §1 (pp. 294–297), Theorem 1.3
88	(Riemann–Roch) and Example 1.3.5 (genus-`0` specialisation). Stacks Project

92	set_option autoImplicit false
93	
94	universe u
95	
96	open CategoryTheory Limits
97	
98	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
99	
100	/-! ## §1. The Euler characteristic of a coherent sheaf on a curve -/
101	
102	/-- **Euler characteristic of a `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf on a smooth proper
103	curve `C / k̄`.**
104	
105	On a curve (one-dimensional scheme), Grothendieck vanishing
106	(Hartshorne III.2.7) gives `H^i(C, 𝓕) = 0` for `i ≥ 2`, so the classical
107	alternating sum
108	`χ(𝓕) = Σ_{i ≥ 0} (-1)^i dim_{k̄} H^i(C, 𝓕)` collapses to the two-term
109	expression
110	`χ(𝓕) = dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝓕) − dim_{k̄} H¹(C, 𝓕)`.
111	
112	This is the definition we ship. Coherence of `𝓕` on the proper `k̄`-scheme
113	`C` guarantees that both `H⁰` and `H¹` are finite-dimensional `k̄`-vector
114	spaces (Serre's coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem, the same
115	finiteness backing `AlgebraicGeometry.genus`), so the two `Module.finrank`s
116	are honest natural numbers and the difference is a well-defined integer.
117	
118	The `Module k̄`-valued cohomology pipeline is the project's
119	`Scheme.HModule` (iter-009), the same wrapper used by
120	`AlgebraicGeometry.genus`.
121	
122	Blueprint reference: `def:eulerChar_curve`
123	(Hartshorne IV.1 p. 295, displayed inside the proof of Theorem 1.3). -/
124	noncomputable def Scheme.eulerCharacteristic
125	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
126	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
127	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
128	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
129	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
130	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar)) : ℤ :=
131	  (Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar F 0) : ℤ)
132	    - (Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar F 1) : ℤ)
133	
134	/-! ## §2. The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor (placeholder)
135	
136	The honest construction of `𝒪_C(D)` lives in the sibling chapter `RR.3`
137	(`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`), where the locally-principal ideal sheaves of
138	closed points are glued into an invertible `𝒪_C`-module. To keep the
139	iter-174 skeleton's pin signatures substantive, we expose `𝒪_C(D)` as a
140	typed-`sorry` `ModuleCat k̄`-valued sheaf-on-`C`. The iter-175+ closure of
141	`RR.3` will replace the body with the construction; downstream pins (the
142	`ℓ`-invariant, the χ-identity, the genus-`0` Riemann–Roch formula) consume
143	this placeholder only through its `H⁰` and `H¹`, so their signatures
144	encode arithmetic identities on finiteness-of-cohomology outputs and are
145	not vacuous. -/
146	
147	namespace Scheme.WeilDivisor
148	
149	variable {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
150	  {C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} [IsProper C.hom]
151	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
152	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
153	
154	/-- **The invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper
155	curve `C / k̄`** (placeholder for `RR.3`).
156	
157	For a Weil divisor `D = Σᵢ nᵢ · [Pᵢ]` on a smooth proper geometrically
158	irreducible curve `C / k̄`, the associated invertible sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` is the
159	locally-free `𝒪_C`-module of rank one cut out (locally near each `Pᵢ`) by
160	the `(−nᵢ)`-th power of the local uniformiser at `Pᵢ`. Hartshorne II §6
161	Proposition 6.13 packages this construction; the project-side `RR.3`
162	chapter (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`, to be added) will produce the carrier as
163	a `Sheaf` of `ModuleCat k̄`-modules on `C.left`.
164	
165	In the iter-174 skeleton this is a typed-`sorry`. The body in `RR.3` will
166	be the invertible-sheaf glue of the locally principal ideals of the
167	support of `D`. -/
168	noncomputable def sheafOf (_D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
169	    Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
170	      (ModuleCat.{u} kbar) :=
171	  sorry
172	
173	/-! ## §3. The `ℓ`-invariant of a Weil divisor -/
174	
175	/-- **The `ℓ`-invariant of a Weil divisor `D` on a smooth proper curve
176	`C / k̄`.**
177	
178	By definition,
179	`ℓ(D) := dim_{k̄} H⁰(C, 𝒪_C(D)) ∈ ℕ`,
180	the `k̄`-dimension of the space of global sections of the invertible sheaf
181	`𝒪_C(D)` associated to `D`. Finiteness of `ℓ(D)` is a consequence of the
182	coherent-cohomology finiteness theorem on a proper `k̄`-scheme
183	(Hartshorne II.5.19 / III.5.2), the same input that backs the finiteness
184	of `genus C`.
185	
186	The classical *complete linear system* `|D|` has `k̄`-projective dimension
187	`ℓ(D) − 1`; the chapter never uses this projective interpretation —
188	`ℓ(D)` is the only quantity consumed by the Hartshorne IV.1.3.5 chain.
189	
190	Blueprint reference: `def:l_invariant` (Hartshorne IV.1 p. 295,
191	"We denote `dim_k H⁰(X, 𝒪(D))` by `l(D)`"). -/
192	noncomputable def l (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) : ℕ :=
193	  Module.finrank kbar (Scheme.HModule kbar (sheafOf (C := C) D) 0)
194	
195	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
196	
197	/-! ## §4. The χ-identity: `χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g`
198	
199	The bridge from the structural Euler characteristic to the arithmetic
200	degree of a divisor. Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3 reduces Riemann–Roch to
201	this identity; the proof is the inductive `D ↔ D + [P]` step (additivity
202	of χ on the closed-point short exact sequence, with base case `D = 0`
203	giving `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` from `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` and the definition of
204	the genus). The closure of the body is iter-175+ work after the
205	`Euler-characteristic additivity on a short exact sequence`-style
206	project-side helper is supplied and the `RR.3` sheaf `𝒪_C(D)` has a
207	body. -/
208	
209	/-- **Euler-characteristic identity for `𝒪_C(D)` on a smooth proper curve
210	of genus `g`.**
211	
212	For every Weil divisor `D ∈ Div(C)`,
213	`χ(𝒪_C(D)) = deg(D) + 1 − g(C)`.
214	
215	The proof is Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3's reduction: induction on the
216	free-abelian-group structure of `Div(C)` on closed points, base case `D =
217	0` giving `χ(𝒪_C) = 1 − g` (the `dim H⁰(C, 𝒪_C) = 1` is the Hartshorne
218	I.3.4 input via the project's `H⁰`-bridge), and inductive step via
219	additivity of χ on the closed-point short exact sequence
220	`0 → 𝒪_C(D) → 𝒪_C(D + [P]) → k(P) → 0`.
221	
222	Blueprint reference: `thm:euler_char_eq_deg_plus_one_minus_genus`
223	(Hartshorne IV.1 Theorem 1.3, p. 295). -/
224	theorem Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus
225	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
226	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
227	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
228	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
229	    (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) :
230	    Scheme.eulerCharacteristic C (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D)
231	      = (Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D) + 1 - (AlgebraicGeometry.genus C : ℤ) :=
232	  sorry
233	
234	/-! ## §5. The Riemann–Roch formula in genus zero -/
235	
236	/-- **Riemann–Roch in genus zero (Hartshorne IV.1 Example 1.3.5).**
237	
238	Let `C` be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over the
239	algebraically closed field `k̄` with `g(C) = 0`, and let `D ∈ Div(C)` with
240	`deg D ≥ 0`. Then
241	`ℓ(D) = deg(D) + 1`.
242	
243	The proof specialises the χ-identity
244	`eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus` to `g = 0`,
245	unfolds `χ` via `def:eulerChar_curve`, and absorbs the `H¹`-vanishing
246	hypothesis (named premise `_hH1`: `H¹(C, 𝒪_C(D)) = 0`, which is the
247	`H¹`-vanishing of a non-negative-degree invertible sheaf on a
248	genus-`0` curve, to be discharged by `RR.3` once `𝒪_C(D)` has a body and
249	the cohomology of `𝒪_{ℙ¹}(d)` is computed).
250	
251	Blueprint reference: `thm:riemannRoch_genus_zero` (Hartshorne IV.1
252	Example 1.3.5, p. 297). -/
253	theorem Scheme.WeilDivisor.l_eq_degree_plus_one_of_genus_zero
254	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
255	    (C : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) [IsProper C.hom]
256	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
257	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] [IsIntegral C.left]
258	    (D : C.left.WeilDivisor) (_hg : AlgebraicGeometry.genus C = 0)
259	    (_hdeg : (0 : ℤ) ≤ Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D)
260	    (_hH1 : Module.finrank kbar
261	      (Scheme.HModule kbar (Scheme.WeilDivisor.sheafOf (C := C) D) 1) = 0) :
262	    (Scheme.WeilDivisor.l (C := C) D : ℤ)
263	      = Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree D + 1 := by
264	  have h := Scheme.eulerCharacteristic_eq_degree_plus_one_minus_genus C D
265	  simp only [Scheme.eulerCharacteristic, _hg, _hH1,
266	    Nat.cast_zero, sub_zero] at h
267	  exact h
268	
269	end AlgebraicGeometry

8	/-!
9	# The Quot scheme (A.2.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` — a projective `S`-scheme representing the functor of
15	`T`-flat coherent quotients of `E_T` on `X_T = X ×_S T` with Hilbert
16	polynomial `Φ` on every fiber — together with the in-project sub-build for
17	the Grassmannian *scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a
18	linear-algebra Grassmannian).
19	
20	## Status (iter-176 Lane H file-skeleton — re-dispatch)
21	
22	iter-175 Lane H died to the Anthropic session-limit reset window without
23	ever calling `Write` (the file was never created). iter-176 re-dispatches
24	the file-skeleton verbatim. Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations
25	carries the *intended* substantive type signature (matching the
26	`\lean{...}` pin in `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`) with a
27	`sorry` body. The bodies are iter-177+ work; the substantive proofs are
28	deep (Nitsure §5: boundedness ⟶ Grassmannian embedding ⟶ flattening
29	stratification ⟶ valuative criterion).
30	
31	The 6 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
32	
33	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
34	   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
35	   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
36	   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.
37	
38	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def, ~6 LOC) — the **Quot
39	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
40	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
41	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
42	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
43	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
44	
45	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (def, ~5 LOC) — the
46	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
47	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.
48	
49	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` (theorem, ~8 LOC)
50	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
51	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
52	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
53	
54	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotScheme` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
55	   **Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman representability theorem** for the Quot
56	   functor: for a noetherian `S`, a projective `π : X ⟶ S`, a relatively
57	   very ample `L` on `X`, a coherent `E`, and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the functor
58	   `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` is representable by a projective `S`-scheme.
59	
60	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChangeCohomology` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the
61	   **flat base-change theorem of cohomology** (Stacks tag 02KH): for a
62	   cartesian square with `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact quasi-separated,
63	   the canonical base-change map `g* (f_* F) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* F)` is an
64	   isomorphism. The current scaffold encodes the `i = 0` direct-image
65	   form (substantive content of (ii) of the Stacks 02KH statement); the
66	   `R^i f_*` form for `i ≥ 1` requires the higher-direct-image
67	   infrastructure not present at the pinned commit.
68	
69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
72	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
75	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
76	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
77	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
78	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
79	- `Grassmannian.representable` and `QuotScheme` package the
80	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
81	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
82	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
83	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
84	  that isomorphism family).
85	- `flatBaseChangeCohomology` produces a `Nonempty (... ≅ ...)` of an
86	  isomorphism between two `S'`-modules built via the pullback/pushforward
87	  bifunctor; the iso is non-trivial (it is `Stacks 02KH` content, not
88	  the identity-on-the-same-object iso `Iso.refl _`).
89	
90	## Mathlib status
91	
92	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides:
93	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` (the category `X.Modules`),
94	- `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` (the
95	  pullback–pushforward adjunction at level `i = 0`),
96	- `CategoryTheory.IsPullback` for cartesian squares,
97	- `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy` for representable functors,
98	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat`, `AlgebraicGeometry.QuasiCompact`,
99	  `AlgebraicGeometry.QuasiSeparated`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper`,
100	  `AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocallyNoetherian`
101	  (morphism / object property predicates), and
102	- `Polynomial` for `ℚ[λ]`.
103	
104	Mathlib does NOT provide (at the pinned commit):
105	- a Grassmannian *scheme* (only a linear-algebra Grassmannian
106	  as a finite-rank-quotient variety),
107	- a `IsProjective` morphism property,
108	- the Quot/Hilbert functor or its representability,
109	- `R^i f_*` higher direct images on `Scheme.Modules`,
110	- Castelnuovo–Mumford `m`-regularity,
111	- Snapper's Lemma for the polynomial property of Euler characteristics.
112	
113	The current file-skeleton uses `IsProper π` as the structural stand-in
114	for "projective `π`" (every projective morphism is proper; the
115	restriction is harmless in the Route A consumer setting where `π` comes
116	from a smooth proper curve, which is automatically projective).
117	iter-177+ refinement: once Mathlib gains an `IsProjective` morphism
118	property, the hypothesis tightens.
119

133	open CategoryTheory Limits

135	namespace AlgebraicGeometry

137	namespace Scheme
138	
139	/-! ## §1. Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf
140	
141	For a finite-type morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with `S` noetherian and a coherent
142	sheaf `F` on `X` whose schematic support is proper over `S` (here encoded
143	as plain `X.Modules` for the file-skeleton), the per-fiber Hilbert
144	polynomial is the function
145	
146	`s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ],   Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`.
147	
148	Snapper's Lemma ensures this is a polynomial in `m`; the proof requires
149	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure and is not stated here.
150	
151	Blueprint reference: `def:hilbert_polynomial` (Nitsure §1; cf. Hartshorne
152	III.5.2). -/
153	
154	/-- The **Hilbert polynomial** of a coherent sheaf `F` on `X` over `S` at
155	the fiber over `s ∈ S` with respect to a line bundle `L` on `X`.
156	
157	Encoded as a function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]`. The defining formula
158	
159	`Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗ m})
160	            = Σ_i (-1)^i dim_{κ(s)} H^i(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`
161	
162	is a polynomial in `m` by Snapper's Lemma; the polynomial coefficients
163	depend on `s` through the fiber `F|_{X_s}`. When `F` is `S`-flat the
164	function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s}` is locally constant on `S`.
165	
166	iter-177+: the body unfolds to the graded-Euler-characteristic
167	construction once `χ` of a coherent sheaf on a noetherian scheme +
168	Snapper's polynomial-eventually-property are in scope. For the iter-176
169	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
170	noncomputable def hilbertPolynomial {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
171	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _F : X.Modules) (_s : S) :
172	    Polynomial ℚ :=
173	  sorry
174	
175	/-! ## §2. The Quot functor
176	
177	The Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` sends an `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the
178	set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` where
179	- `F` is a coherent sheaf on `X_T = X ×_S T` whose schematic support is
180	  proper over `T` and which is `T`-flat,
181	- `q : E_T ↠ F` is a surjective `O_{X_T}`-linear homomorphism,
182	- the fiberwise Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` with respect to `L|_{X_t}`
183	  equals `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
184	
185	Two pairs `(F, q)` and `(F', q')` are equivalent iff `ker(q) = ker(q')`.
186	
187	The Hilbert scheme is the special case `E = O_X`:
188	`Hilb^{Φ,L}_{X/S} = Quot^{Φ,L}_{O_X/X/S}`.
189	
190	Blueprint reference: `def:quot_functor` (Nitsure §1; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
191	
192	/-- The **Quot functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of coherent quotients of `E`
193	on `X ×_S -` with Hilbert polynomial `Φ`.
194	
195	Encoded as a contravariant functor `(Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u`, sending an
196	`S`-scheme `T → S` (i.e. an object of `Over S`) to the set of
197	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` of a `T`-flat coherent
198	sheaf `F` on `X ×_S T` with proper support and a surjection
199	`q : E_T ↠ F` whose fiberwise Hilbert polynomial is `Φ`, modulo
200	`ker(q) = ker(q')`. Functoriality is pullback of the quotient along
201	`X ×_S T' ⟶ X ×_S T`.
202	
203	iter-177+: the body packages the on-objects / on-morphisms data using the
204	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` bifunctor on the relative product
205	`X ×_S T`, with the equivalence relation `ker(q) = ker(q')` quotiented
206	out via `Setoid` / `Quotient`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body
207	is a typed `sorry`. -/
208	noncomputable def QuotFunctor {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
209	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _E : X.Modules)

216	/-! ## §3. The Grassmannian scheme
217	
218	Since Mathlib carries no Grassmannian *scheme*, we encode it here as a
219	contravariant functor on `Over S` together with a representability
220	statement. The construction proceeds by gluing `binom(r, d)` affine
221	charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the Plücker cocycle, yielding a smooth
222	projective `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`,
223	equipped with a tautological rank-`d` quotient
224	`π* V ↠ U` and the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
225	
226	Blueprint references: `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
227	`thm:grassmannian_representable` (Nitsure §1 Exercise (2),
228	"Construction of Grassmannian"; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
229	
230	namespace Scheme
231	
232	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
233	rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r ≥ d`.
234	
235	Encoded as the functor sending an `S`-scheme `T → S` to the set of
236	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with
237	`q : V_T ↠ F` a surjection of `O_T`-modules and `F` locally free of
238	rank `d`, modulo `ker(q) = ker(q')`. Concretely
239	`Grass(V, d) = Quot^{d, O_S}_{V/S/S}` (the Quot functor for `X = S`,
240	`E = V`, constant Hilbert polynomial `d`).
241	
242	iter-177+: the body re-exports `QuotFunctor (𝟙 S) (?) V Φ_d`, where
243	`Φ_d : Polynomial ℚ` is the constant polynomial `d`. For the iter-176
244	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
245	noncomputable def Grassmannian {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
246	    (_V : S.Modules) (_d : ℕ) :
247	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
248	  sorry
249	
250	/-- **Representability of the Grassmannian.**
251	
252	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r`,
253	and `1 ≤ d ≤ r`, the Grassmannian functor `Grass(V, d)` of
254	`Grassmannian` is representable by a smooth projective `S`-scheme
255	`Gr_S(V, d) ⟶ S` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with a
256	tautological rank-`d` quotient `π* V ↠ U`. The determinant line bundle
257	`det(U)` is relatively very ample, giving a Plücker closed embedding
258	`Gr_S(V, d) ↪ ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
259	
260	We package the conclusion as the existence of a representing
261	`Y : Over S` together with a `Functor.RepresentableBy Y` witness for
262	`Grassmannian V d`; the additional projective / smooth / Plücker
263	structure is implicit in the construction and is iter-177+ refinement
264	work (once the proof body lands).
265	
266	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §1 "Construction of Grassmannian":
267	glue the `binom(r, d)` affine charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the
268	Plücker cocycle, verify separatedness via the diagonal cut, verify
269	properness by the DVR valuative criterion, build the tautological
270	quotient `U`, exhibit the Plücker embedding via the determinant line
271	bundle. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
272	theorem Grassmannian.representable {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
273	    (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
274	    ∃ (Y : Over S), Nonempty ((Grassmannian V d).RepresentableBy Y) := by
275	  sorry

277	/-! ## §4. Representability of the Quot scheme
278	
279	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman: for a noetherian `S`, a projective
280	`π : X ⟶ S`, a relatively very ample `L` on `X`, a coherent
281	`E` on `X`, and `Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`, the Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` is
282	representable by a *projective* `S`-scheme.
283	
284	The proof has four steps (Nitsure §5):
285	1. **Boundedness** via Castelnuovo–Mumford `m`-regularity (uniform across
286	   fibers of `π` and across coherent quotients of `E_s` with Hilbert
287	   polynomial `Φ`).
288	2. **Grassmannian embedding**
289	   `α : Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} ↪ Grass(W ⊗_{O_S} Sym^r V, Φ(r))`
290	   for `r ≥ m`, sending `⟨F, q⟩ ↦ ⟨(π_T)_* F(r), (π_T)_*(q(r))⟩`.
291	3. **Locally closed in Grassmannian** via the flattening stratification
292	   applied to the universal cokernel on the Grassmannian, producing the
293	   stratum `T_0^Φ`.
294	4. **Closed embedding** by the valuative criterion of properness for
295	   DVRs.
296	
297	The reduction to the universal case `X = ℙ(V)`, `E = π*W` is recorded as
298	`lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W` in the blueprint chapter.
299	
300	Blueprint reference: `thm:quot_representable` (Nitsure §5; FGA Explained
301	Ch. 5; Grothendieck, FGA TDTE-IV). -/
302	
303	/-- **Representability of the Quot scheme** (Grothendieck, Altman–Kleiman).
304	
305	Let `S` be a noetherian scheme, `π : X ⟶ S` a projective morphism (here
306	encoded as a proper `LocallyOfFiniteType` morphism; the projectivity
307	upgrades once `IsProjective` lands in Mathlib), `L` a line bundle on `X`
308	(relatively very ample), `E` a coherent `O_X`-module, and
309	`Φ ∈ ℚ[λ]`. Then the Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of `QuotFunctor`
310	is representable by an `S`-scheme.
311	
312	We package the conclusion as the existence of `Q : Over S` together with
313	a `Functor.RepresentableBy Q` witness for `QuotFunctor π L E Φ`; the
314	*projectivity* of `Q ⟶ S` (and the universal quotient
315	`q^univ : π^*_Q E ↠ F^univ` on `X ×_S Q^{Φ,L}`) is implicit in the
316	construction (Plücker-embedded into a projective Grassmannian over `S`)
317	and is iter-177+ refinement work.
318	
319	iter-177+: the body follows the four-step Nitsure §5 proof
320	(boundedness ⟶ Grassmannian embedding ⟶ flattening stratification ⟶
321	valuative-criterion closed embedding); the sub-lemmas live in
322	`lem:quot_boundedness`, `lem:quot_alpha_injective`,
323	`lem:quot_valuative_criterion`, and the existential reduction in
324	`lem:quot_reduction_to_pi_star_W`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the
325	body is a typed `sorry`. -/
326	theorem QuotScheme {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
327	    (π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType π] [IsProper π]
328	    (L E : X.Modules) (Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
329	    ∃ (Q : Over S), Nonempty ((QuotFunctor π L E Φ).RepresentableBy Q) := by
330	  sorry
331	
332	end Scheme

334	/-! ## §5. Cohomology and base change
335	
336	The Quot construction uses cohomology-and-base-change in two places: the
337	boundedness step (Nitsure §5 "Use of m-Regularity") and the Grassmannian
338	embedding (Nitsure §5 "Embedding Quot into Grassmannian"). We record the
339	relevant statement as a named theorem so the Lean encoding can cite it
340	directly.
341	
342	The Stacks 02KH form is the statement for higher direct images
343	`R^i f_*` on quasi-coherent sheaves; for the iter-176 file-skeleton we
344	state the `i = 0` form on `Scheme.Modules`, which is the substantive
345	content of `lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology(ii)` of Stacks 02KH. The
346	`R^i` form is iter-177+ work after higher-direct-image infrastructure
347	is in scope.
348	
349	Blueprint reference: `thm:flat_base_change_cohomology` (Stacks 02KH). -/
350	
351	/-! ### Flat base change of cohomology (Stacks tag 02KH, `i = 0` form)
352	
353	Let
354	```
355	   g'
356	X' ───→ X
357	│       │
358	f'      f
359	↓       ↓
360	S' ───→ S
361	   g
362	```
363	be a cartesian square of schemes with `g` flat and `f` quasi-compact
364	quasi-separated. Let `F` be a sheaf of `O_X`-modules. Then the canonical
365	base-change map `g* (f_* F) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* F)` is an isomorphism in
366	`S'.Modules`.
367	
368	(The full Stacks 02KH statement covers all higher direct images
369	`R^i f_* F` for `i ≥ 0`; the `i = 0` form encoded here is the
370	substantive content of `lemma-flat-base-change-cohomology(ii)` of
371	Stacks 02KH, with the `i ≥ 1` form post-iter-177 work after the
372	higher-direct-image bifunctor lands.)
373	
374	iter-177 (Lane QS-FLAT): the body constructs the canonical base-change
375	natural transformation via the mate equivalence of the
376	`pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunction (Mathlib's `mateEquiv` of
377	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction`), then exhibits the iso
378	via the `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso` helper. The deep mathematical
379	content (Stacks tag 02KH / 02KE / 00H8) lives entirely in the helper;
380	it reduces affine-locally to: for a flat ring map `A → B` and an
381	`A`-algebra `R`, the canonical map `B ⊗_A H^i(X, F) → H^i(X_B, F_B)`
382	is an iso for any quasi-coherent `F` (the `i = 0` form is what we use).
383	The helper remains a typed `sorry` pending the affine-local reduction
384	+ algebraic flat base change; this is iter-178+ body work after
385	quasi-compact open-cover Mayer-Vietoris infrastructure is in scope. -/
386	
387	/-- The canonical base-change natural transformation `g* (f_* -) ⟶ f'_* ((g')* -)`
388	associated to a cartesian square
389	```
390	     g'
391	X' ─────→ X
392	│         │
393	f'        f
394	↓         ↓
395	S' ─────→ S
396	     g
397	```
398	in `Scheme`. Constructed as the *mate* (Beck–Chevalley transform)
399	under the `pullback ⊣ pushforward` adjunctions on sheaves of modules
400	of the canonical 2-isomorphism
401	`pullback g ⋙ pullback f' ≅ pullback f ⋙ pullback g'` coming from
402	`g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`.
403	
404	This natural transformation always exists (no flatness needed). The
405	content of the flat base-change theorem (Stacks tag 02KH) is the
406	*isomorphism* claim under the hypotheses
407	`[QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]`; that claim is the
408	helper `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso`. -/
409	noncomputable def canonicalBaseChangeMap
410	    {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
411	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
412	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g) :
413	    Scheme.Modules.pushforward f ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pullback g ⟶
414	      Scheme.Modules.pullback g' ⋙ Scheme.Modules.pushforward f' :=
415	  CategoryTheory.mateEquiv
416	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)
417	      (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f')
418	      (((Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp f' g) ≪≫
419	        Scheme.Modules.pullbackCongr sq.w.symm ≪≫
420	        (Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp g' f).symm).hom)

422	/-- **Affine-open form of flat base change** (Stacks tag 00H8 / 02KE).
423	
424	Restriction of `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso` to the case where the
425	open `U ⊆ S'` is affine. On an affine open `U = Spec B ⊆ S'` lying over
426	an affine open `V = Spec A ⊆ S` (chosen so that `g⁻¹V ⊇ U`), the
427	section of `(pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F)` over `U`
428	identifies with the algebraic tensor product
429	`B ⊗_A Γ(f⁻¹V, F)`, while the section of
430	`(pushforward f').obj ((pullback g').obj F)` over `U` identifies with
431	`Γ((f')⁻¹U, (g')*F)`. The canonical map between them is the algebraic
432	flat base-change map of `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` (Mathlib's
433	`Module.Flat.isBaseChange`) for the flat ring map `A → B` arising from
434	`Flat g`.
435	
436	iter-180 Lane F: helper-with-substantive-Mathlib-gap. The body is a typed
437	`sorry` carrying the *intended* mathematical content (Stacks 02KE / 00H8
438	for cohomology, here in the `H⁰` form). Concretely the body would (a)
439	choose an affine `V ⊆ S` covering the image of `U` under `g`, (b)

447	    {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}

449	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g)

451	    (F : X.Modules) (U : S'.Opens) (_hU : IsAffineOpen U) :

471	/-- **Open-cover gluing for the section-wise flat base change**
472	(Mayer-Vietoris reduction, Stacks 02KH(ii) corollary).
473	
474	If the section of the canonical base-change map is an iso over *every*
475	affine open `V ⊆ S'`, then it is an iso over every open `U ⊆ S'` as well.
476	This is the standard Mayer-Vietoris descent argument for a morphism of
477	quasi-coherent sheaves on the base: pick an affine cover of `U`, the
478	morphism is an iso on each chart, hence iso on `U` by gluing along the
479	intersections (which are quasi-compact thanks to `QuasiSeparated f`).

481	iter-180 Lane F: helper-with-substantive-Mathlib-gap. The body is a typed
482	`sorry` carrying the *intended* descent argument. Required ingredients
483	(not yet in scope at the pinned Mathlib commit):
484	* the basis property of affine opens (`Scheme.affineOpenCover`);
485	* iso-on-basis ⟹ iso-on-open for sheaves of modules
486	  (`Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_basis`, project-side helper);
487	* a Mayer-Vietoris on pushforwards via `QuasiSeparated f`. -/
488	private theorem canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_affineCover
489	    {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
490	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
491	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g)
492	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]
493	    (F : X.Modules)
494	    (h_affine : ∀ V : S'.Opens, IsAffineOpen V →
495	        IsIso (((canonicalBaseChangeMap sq).app F).app V))
496	    (U : S'.Opens) :
497	    IsIso (((canonicalBaseChangeMap sq).app F).app U) := by
498	  -- Mayer-Vietoris descent. Substantive Mathlib gap. Intended body:

503	  --   3. Both `(pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F)` and
504	  --      `(pushforward f').obj ((pullback g').obj F)` are sheaves of

516	  -- (The dependence on `QuasiSeparated f` enters in step 3 above: it

522	
523	For every open `U` of `S'`, the section over `U` of the canonical base-change
524	map `(pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F) ⟶ (pushforward f').obj ((pullback g').obj F)`

527	This is the substantive content of Stacks 02KH(ii) (the `i = 0` form), and
528	splits cleanly into two named substantive Mathlib gaps:
529	* `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen` — the affine case
530	  via algebraic flat base change `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` (Stacks 00H8 /
531	  02KE);
532	* `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_affineCover` — the descent from
533	  affine opens to arbitrary opens via Mayer-Vietoris on the quasi-separated
534	  morphism `f`.

536	The body of this theorem composes the two helpers cleanly; the substantive
537	content has been factored into the helper bodies. -/
538	theorem canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
539	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
540	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g)
541	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]
542	    (F : X.Modules) (U : S'.Opens) :
543	    IsIso (((canonicalBaseChangeMap sq).app F).app U) :=
544	  -- Composition of the two named substantive helpers: the affine-open case
545	  -- via `Module.Flat.isBaseChange`, then the Mayer-Vietoris descent.
546	  canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_affineCover sq F
547	    (fun V hV => canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen sq F V hV)
548	    U
549	
550	/-- **Flat base-change is an isomorphism** (Stacks tag 02KH, `i = 0`).
551	
552	The canonical base-change natural transformation `canonicalBaseChangeMap`
553	is an isomorphism at every coherent sheaf `F` under the hypotheses
554	`[QuasiCompact f]`, `[QuasiSeparated f]`, `[Flat g]`.
555	
556	The proof reduces section-wise via `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`
557	to the section-form helper `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso`,
558	which captures Stacks 02KH(ii) — the substantive algebraic content
559	(`Module.Flat.isBaseChange` on each affine open + Mayer-Vietoris for
560	quasi-separated `f`). -/
561	theorem canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
562	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
563	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g)
564	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]
565	    (F : X.Modules) :
566	    IsIso ((canonicalBaseChangeMap sq).app F) :=
567	  Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app.mpr
568	    (fun U => canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso sq F U)
569	
570	theorem flatBaseChangeCohomology {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}}
571	    {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
572	    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g)
573	    [QuasiCompact f] [QuasiSeparated f] [Flat g]
574	    (F : X.Modules) :
575	    Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj
576	                ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f).obj F) ≅
577	              (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f').obj
578	                ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F)) :=
579	  -- Build the canonical Beck-Chevalley base-change map and wrap it in `asIso`
580	  -- using the iso-claim from `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso`.
581	  ⟨@asIso _ _ _ _ _ (canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso sq F)⟩

6	import Mathlib

9	/-!
10	# Milne Theorem 3.2: a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian
11	variety extends (A.4.c)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.c** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
14	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the single
15	theorem of Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3, Theorem 3.2:
16	**a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an abelian variety extends,
17	uniquely, to a regular morphism**. This is the input that the Albanese
18	universal-property build (A.4.d, `Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`) consumes when
19	promoting the symmetric-product rational map
20	`C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism `J → A`.
21	
22	Milne's proof is two lines: combine Theorem 3.1 (the codim-$\geq 2$ extension
23	result for rational maps from a normal variety to a complete variety —
24	project-side `thm:codim_one_extension` of
25	`Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, A.4.a) with Lemma 3.3 (the
26	pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy structure for rational maps into a group variety
27	— project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` of the same chapter). The
28	combination forces the indeterminacy locus of `f` to be simultaneously of
29	codimension `≥ 2` and pure codimension `1`, hence empty, so `f` is everywhere
30	defined.
31	
32	## Status (iter-175 Lane J file-skeleton)
33	
34	This file is the **iter-175 Lane J** file-skeleton: the single pinned
35	declaration carries the intended substantive type signature (matching the

46	   — Milne Theorem 3.2: for `X` a smooth (= nonsingular) variety over an

51	## Note on type expressivity
52	
53	The signature is encoded against Mathlib's existing
54	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` (a partial-map equivalence class on
55	dense opens) and `Scheme.Hom.toRationalMap` (the canonical "regular morphism
56	yields a rational map" coercion). The substantive claim is the existence
57	and uniqueness of a `g : X.left ⟶ A.left` with `g.toRationalMap = f`. The
58	type is non-tautological: the existence is the content of Milne 3.2;
59	uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle

62	## Abelian-variety conventions
63	
64	Throughout the project (cf. `AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`),
65	an **abelian variety over `k̄`** is encoded as an object
66	`A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying the four instances:
67	
68	- `[GrpObj A]` (group-object structure on the over-category),
69	- `[IsProper A.hom]` (complete),
70	- `[Smooth A.hom]` (nonsingular),
71	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` (geometrically irreducible).

73	A **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** (in the convention of
74	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1) is an
75	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:

77	- `[Smooth X.hom]`,
78	- `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
79	- `[IsSeparated X.hom]`,
80	- `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
81	- `[IsIntegral X.left]`,
82	- `[IsReduced X.left]`.
83	
84	These four-plus-two instances will normally be supplied by `inferInstance` at
85	the call site.

106	/-! ## Milne Theorem 3.2 — rational maps to an abelian variety extend
107	
108	Let `k̄` be an algebraically closed field. Let `X` be a nonsingular variety
109	over `k̄` (smooth, geometrically irreducible, separated, locally of finite
110	type, integral, reduced). Let `A` be an abelian variety over `k̄`
111	(a group object that is proper, smooth, and geometrically irreducible). For
112	every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` there is a *unique* regular morphism
113	`g : X ⟶ A` whose induced rational map equals `f`.
114	
115	Milne's argument (`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` §1.4, Theorem 3.2):
116	let `U ⊆ X` be a maximal open of definition of `f` and `Z := X ∖ U`.
117	By the codim-$\geq 2$ extension theorem (Theorem 3.1, project-side
118	`thm:codim_one_extension`) the closed subset `Z` has codimension `≥ 2`.
119	By the pure-codim-$1$ indeterminacy lemma for maps into a group variety
120	(Lemma 3.3, project-side `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy`), `Z` is either
121	empty or of pure codimension `1`. Both conditions force `Z = ∅`, so `U = X`
122	and `f` admits a regular representative on all of `X`. Uniqueness is the
123	standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle (cf.
124	`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two regular morphisms `X ⟶ A` that
125	agree on a dense open of `X` agree everywhere.
126	
127	Blueprint reference: `thm:rational_map_to_av_extends` (Milne, *Abelian
128	Varieties*, Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17). The pin in the chapter is at
129	`Albanese_Thm32RationalMapExtension.tex` line 50.
130	
131	iter-176+ proof skeleton:
132	1. Pick a `PartialMap` representative `φ : X.PartialMap A` of `f` (using
133	   `Scheme.RationalMap.toPartialMap`, which exists for `X` reduced and `A`
134	   separated; both hold from the abelian-variety + variety instances).
135	2. Show `φ.domain = ⊤` by combining `thm:codim_one_extension` (codim `≥ 2`
136	   complement) with `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (complement empty or
137	   pure codim `1`).
138	3. Read off `g := φ.hom` (the morphism on the full domain) and verify
139	   `g.toRationalMap = f`.
140	4. Uniqueness: two `g₁, g₂ : X ⟶ A` with `g₁.toRationalMap = g₂.toRationalMap`
141	   agree on a dense open of `X`, so by
142	   `AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` and reducedness of `X` they agree
143	   everywhere. -/
144	
145	/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (1/2): integrality of the abelian variety.**
146	
147	For an abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄` —
148	encoded in the project as `A : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying
149	`[GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]` —
150	the underlying scheme `A.left` is integral.
151	
152	**Derivation.** The proof has three steps:
153	
154	1. `Spec k̄` is a singleton scheme (Mathlib instance
155	   `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf` provides `Unique`,
156	   which yields both `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty`).
157	2. `GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom + Spec k̄ a single point ⟹ IrreducibleSpace A.left`
158	   via Mathlib's `GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton`.
159	3. `IrreducibleSpace + IsReduced ⟹ IsIntegral` via Mathlib's
160	   `isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced`.
161	
162	The substantive Mathlib gap is the implication `Smooth A.hom ⟹ IsReduced A.left`
163	(at the project's pinned commit, Mathlib's `Smooth` morphism class does not
164	ship a `IsReduced` consequence for the source over a reduced base). The
165	gap is isolated to the single `IsReduced A.left` hypothesis in the proof,
166	which is left as a `sorry`; all other steps close axiom-clean.
167	
168	When Mathlib ships the bridge (Stacks `034V` / `02G4` over a field
169	direction), the single `sorry` discharges and this helper becomes
170	axiom-clean automatically.
171	
172	This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor of the iter-179 helper
173	`av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, splitting its `IsIntegral A.left`
174	conjunct into a self-contained named lemma that does not depend on the
175	rational map `f`. -/
176	private theorem av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred
177	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
178	    (A : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
179	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
180	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] :
181	    IsIntegral A.left := by
182	  -- Step 1: `Spec k̄` has a unique point (Mathlib instance
183	  -- `Scheme.instUniqueCarrierCarrierCommRingCatSpecOf`); both
184	  -- `Subsingleton` and `Nonempty` follow.
185	  haveI : Subsingleton (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
186	  haveI : Nonempty (Spec (.of kbar) : Scheme.{u}) := inferInstance
187	  -- Step 2: geometric irreducibility over a singleton base gives `IrreducibleSpace`.
188	  haveI : IrreducibleSpace A.left :=
189	    GeometricallyIrreducible.irreducibleSpace_of_subsingleton A.hom
190	  -- Step 3 (Mathlib gap): smoothness over a field forces reducedness of the
191	  -- source. This is Stacks `034V`/`02G4` over a (reduced) base, but the
192	  -- scheme-level packaging does not ship in Mathlib at the project's pinned
193	  -- commit.
194	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := sorry
195	  exact isIntegral_of_irreducibleSpace_of_isReduced A.left
196	
197	/-- **Iter-180 Lane G split (2/2): codim-1-indeterminacy-freeness from
198	Milne's two-input argument.**
199	
200	For a rational map `f : X ⇢ A` from a nonsingular variety `X` to an
201	abelian variety `A` over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, every
202	codim-1 point of `X` lies in `f.domain` (`CodimOneFree f`).
203	
204	**Derivation (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*, proof of Theorem 3.2, §I.3, p. 17).**
205	Two project-side inputs combine to force the indeterminacy locus empty:
206	
207	1. `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 in
208	   `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): for a rational map from a smooth
209	   integral variety to a *group* variety, the indeterminacy locus is
210	   either empty or pure codim 1.
211	2. The *codim-≥2 conclusion* of Milne Theorem 3.1 (the unbundled half of
212	   `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`):
213	   for a rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety,
214	   the indeterminacy locus has codim `≥ 2`.
215	
216	Combining (1) ∨ (2): pure codim 1 contradicts codim `≥ 2`, so the
217	indeterminacy locus is empty; trivially every point — and in particular
218	every codim-1 point — lies in `f.domain`, so `CodimOneFree f`.
219	
220	**Status of the substantive content.** The codim-≥2 conclusion of
221	Milne 3.1 is not currently exposed by the project as a standalone lemma;
222	it is encapsulated inside `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s body (which
223	is itself `sorry` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`). Once the project
224	exposes a lemma `indeterminacy_codimGe2_of_smooth_of_complete` separating
225	out the codim-≥2 conclusion, this helper closes via the two-line
226	combination above. Until then it is left as a named `sorry` whose
227	docstring exhaustively records the combination strategy, replacing the
228	iter-179 conjunction-level `sorry` with a more targeted one.
229	
230	This is the iter-180 Lane G refactor's split: the `CodimOneFree f`
231	conjunct is split off into a self-contained named lemma with the
232	substantive missing content localised here. -/
233	private theorem av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy
234	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
235	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
236	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
237	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
238	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
239	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
240	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
241	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
242	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
243	    CodimOneFree f := by
244	  -- Strategy (iter-181+ inline): apply `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
245	  -- to obtain the disjunction `Z(f) = ∅ ∨ ∀ x ∈ Z(f), coheight = 1`; in the
246	  -- empty branch, `CodimOneFree` is immediate; in the pure-codim-1 branch,
247	  -- combine with the codim-≥2 conclusion of Milne 3.1 (currently bundled
248	  -- inside `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`'s body — needs to be exposed
249	  -- as a separate lemma) to derive a contradiction at any putative
250	  -- codim-1 point of `Z(f)`. The remaining content is the codim-≥2
251	  -- exposure, which is the iter-181+ Mathlib-gap consult target.
252	  sorry
253	
254	/-- **Iter-179 Lane E + iter-180 Lane G: the two missing project inputs
255	for `extend_to_av`.**
256	
257	The conjunction of the two named iter-180 Lane G splits:
258	`av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred` (the `IsIntegral A.left` conjunct, with
259	its `IsReduced A.left` Mathlib gap isolated) and
260	`av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy` (the `CodimOneFree f` conjunct, with its
261	codim-≥2-from-Milne-3.1 gap isolated).
262	
263	This wrapper preserves the iter-179 call-site API (`extend_to_av` invokes
264	the conjunction in one `obtain`) while routing the proof to the two
265	narrower lemmas. The body itself is axiom-clean — all substantive content
266	is localised in the two split helpers above. -/
267	private theorem av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree
268	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
269	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
270	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
271	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
272	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
273	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
274	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
275	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
276	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
277	    IsIntegral A.left ∧ CodimOneFree f :=
278	  ⟨av_isIntegral_of_smooth_geomIrred A,
279	    av_codimOneFree_of_indeterminacy f⟩
280	
281	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.2 — a rational map from a nonsingular variety to an
282	abelian variety extends, uniquely, to a regular morphism.**
283	
284	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`, and
285	`A` an abelian variety over `k̄`, every rational map `f : X ⇢ A` has a
286	*unique* regular extension `g : X ⟶ A` (i.e. `g.toRationalMap = f`).
287	
288	This is the input the Albanese universal-property build
289	(`Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`, A.4.d) consumes when promoting the
290	symmetric-product rational map `C^{(g)} ⇢ A` to a regular morphism
291	`J → A`.
292	
293	**Iter-179 Lane E body.** The proof is the standard one-line reduction of
294	Milne's two-line argument:
295	
296	1. Apply `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (Milne Theorem 3.1, project-side
297	   `thm:codim_one_extension` in `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`): given a
298	   `CodimOneFree` rational map from a smooth integral variety to a complete
299	   variety, the map extends uniquely to a regular morphism. This requires
300	   the variety-package instances on `A.left` (separated, locally of finite
301	   type, integral, reduced) in addition to the four AV instances supplied
302	   by the caller.
303	
304	2. Discharge `CodimOneFree f` by invoking the helper
305	   `av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree`, which combines
306	   `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` (Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure
307	   codim 1 or empty indeterminacy for grpScheme targets) with the codim-≥2
308	   half of Milne 3.1. Both pieces are documented in that helper's
309	   docstring; the helper carries the single `sorry` that captures the
310	   remaining math content the project spec does not yet expose.
311	
312	3. Materialize the remaining variety-package instances on `A.left`:
313	   `IsSeparated A.hom` from `IsProper A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
314	   `LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom` from `Smooth A.hom` (Mathlib instance),
315	   `IsReduced A.left` from `IsIntegral A.left` (Mathlib instance
316	   `isReduced_of_isIntegral`).
317	
318	The proof body therefore introduces NO new inline `sorry`: all missing
319	content is concentrated in the single named helper above. -/
320	theorem extend_to_av
321	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
322	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
323	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
324	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
325	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
326	    {A : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
327	    [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
328	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom]
329	    (f : X.left.RationalMap A.left) :
330	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ A.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
331	  -- Step 3: materialise the missing variety-package instances on A.left.
332	  haveI : IsSeparated A.hom := inferInstance
333	  haveI : LocallyOfFiniteType A.hom := inferInstance
334	  obtain ⟨hint, hcod⟩ := av_isIntegral_and_codimOneFree f
335	  haveI := hint
336	  haveI : IsReduced A.left := inferInstance
337	  -- Steps 1–2: apply Milne Theorem 3.1 with the `CodimOneFree` discharged.
338	  exact extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth f hcod
339	
340	end RationalMap

342	end Scheme

344	end AlgebraicGeometry

9	# Auslander–Buchsbaum formula (A.4.b)
10	
11	This file is the **A.4.b** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the
13	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula and the corollary "regular local ring
14	⟹ Cohen–Macaulay" that the sibling sub-build **A.4.a** (codim-1 extension of a
15	rational map across a codim-2 closed point on a regular projective surface)
16	consumes.
17	
18	Per STRATEGY.md L30 this row is gated downstream on A.4.a but is independently
19	startable on the Mathlib side: the algebra here is decoupled from the Albanese
20	geometry and lives entirely under `RingTheory.*`. Mathlib at the project's
21	pinned commit (`b80f227`) exposes `IsRegularLocalRing` and the categorical
22	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` on an abelian category (specialised to
23	`ModuleCat R`), as well as the regular-sequence definition
24	`RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`. The depth function `Module.depth`, the
25	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula, and the Cohen–Macaulay predicate are *not* in
26	Mathlib at the pinned commit — they are the new content scaffolded here.
27	
28	## Status (iter-178 update — projectiveDimension closed)
29	
30	This file was originally landed iter-175 (Lane F file-skeleton) with each
31	blueprint-pinned declaration carrying the *intended* substantive type
32	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
33	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex`). The bodies were
34	scheduled for iter-176+ work; the chapter is independent of the rest of
35	Route A's geometric infrastructure, making it a good parallel-work
36	candidate.
37	
38	iter-178 closed `Module.projectiveDimension` kernel-clean as a one-liner
39	re-export of the categorical `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension`. The
40	remaining five typed `sorry` bodies (`depth`, `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`,
41	`depth_of_short_exact`, `auslander_buchsbaum_formula`,
42	`CohenMacaulay.of_regular`) are substantive multi-iter content and stay
43	gated on dedicated body lanes.
44	
45	The 7 pinned declarations are:
46	
47	1. `RingTheory.Module.depth` (noncomputable def, ~6 LOC) — the `I`-depth of a
48	   finite `R`-module as the supremum of lengths of `M`-regular sequences in
49	   `I`, valued in `ℕ∞`.
50	2. `RingTheory.Module.depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — for
51	   `(R, 𝔪)` Noetherian local and `M ≠ 0` finite, `depth(M)` equals the
52	   smallest `i` with `Ext^i_R(κ, M) ≠ 0`. Encoded via the depth-bound iff
53	   `Ext` vanishes below.
54	3. `Module.projectiveDimension` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC) — an
55	   `R`-module-side wrapper for the categorical
56	   `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R M)`. The categorical
57	   version exists in Mathlib `b80f227`; this is the re-export pinned by the
58	   blueprint.
59	4. `RingTheory.depth_of_short_exact` (theorem, ~12 LOC) — the
60	   depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence inequalities (Stacks 00LE).
61	5. `RingTheory.auslander_buchsbaum_formula` (theorem, ~10 LOC) — the formula
62	   `pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)` for a nonzero finite `R`-module of finite
63	   projective dimension over a Noetherian local ring.
64	6. `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay` (class, ~3 LOC) — `IsCohenMacaulayLocalRing R`
65	   encoded as `depth(R) = krullDim R`.
66	7. `RingTheory.CohenMacaulay.of_regular` (theorem, ~6 LOC) — a regular local
67	   ring is Cohen–Macaulay (the consumer-facing input for A.4.a).

69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
72	declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	- `depth` returns `ℕ∞` and is defined by the regular-sequence supremum.
75	- `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` is encoded as the depth-bound `↔` `Ext`
76	  vanishing characterisation (the smallest non-vanishing `Ext` index = depth).
77	- `projectiveDimension` re-exports the categorical
78	  `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` on `ModuleCat.of R M`.
79	- `depth_of_short_exact` packages the three Stacks 00LE inequalities into a
80	  conjunction.
81	- `auslander_buchsbaum_formula` is the numeric equation
82	  `pd + depth = depth(R)`.
83	- `CohenMacaulay` is the equation `depth(R) = ringKrullDim R`.
84	- `CohenMacaulay.of_regular` is an `IsRegularLocalRing → IsCohenMacaulayLocalRing`
85	  implication, the consumer-facing statement.

112	/-! ## §1. Depth of a finite module
113	
114	The `I`-depth of a finite `R`-module `M` is the supremum in
115	`{0, 1, 2, …, ∞}` of the lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in `I`
116	(provided `IM ≠ M`; if `IM = M` we set `depth_I(M) = ∞`). Mathlib `b80f227`
117	exposes the regular-sequence predicate `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`
118	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.RegularSequence`) but not the resulting
119	numeric depth function — that is the gap this declaration fills.
120	
121	Blueprint reference: `def:depth` (Stacks tag 00LF). -/
122	
123	/-- The **`I`-depth** of a finite `R`-module `M`: the supremum (in `ℕ∞`) of
124	lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in the ideal `I`.
125	
126	When `IM = M` (the "trivial-quotient" case, e.g. `M = 0` or `I = R`) the
127	supremum is taken to be `⊤` by convention. When `(R, 𝔪)` is local one usually
128	calls `depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M` simply *the depth* of `M`.
129	
130	iter-176+: the body is the supremum
131	```
132	sSup { (n : ℕ∞) | ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = n ∧ (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ I) ∧
133	                  RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs }
134	```
135	folded with the `IM = M` clause. The signature is non-tautological: it
136	asserts a function `(Ideal R) → (M : Type v) → ℕ∞` matching the Stacks
137	00LF definition.
138	
139	**iter-179 Mathlib-gap check (Lane F Target 2 STRETCH)**: a pinned-commit
140	audit of `Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.Depth` (the only file under
141	`Mathlib/RingTheory/*` containing the word "depth") confirms that Mathlib
142	`b80f227` ships only depth-zero lemmas (`IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff`)
143	and *not* the numeric depth function itself. The one-liner re-export route
144	is therefore not available; the body stays a typed `sorry` until an
145	iter-180+ body lane fills the supremum-with-`IM=M` clause directly. -/
146	noncomputable def depth {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (_I : Ideal R)
147	    (_M : Type v) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : ℕ∞ :=
148	  open Classical in
149	  if _I • (⊤ : Submodule R _M) = ⊤ then (⊤ : ℕ∞)
150	  else sSup { n : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = n ∧
151	    (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ _I) ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular _M rs }
152	
153	end Module
154	
155	end RingTheory

157	/-! ## §2. Projective dimension
158	
159	Mathlib `b80f227` exposes the categorical
160	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension : C → WithBot ℕ∞` on an abelian category
161	with enough projectives (file `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.Projective.Dimension`).
162	For `R`-modules this specialises to `ModuleCat.of R M`. The blueprint pins the
163	`Module.projectiveDimension` name as the re-export that downstream consumers
164	can use directly on an `R`-module without first packaging it in `ModuleCat`.
165	
166	Blueprint reference: `def:projective_dimension`. -/
167	
168	namespace Module
169	
170	/-- The **projective dimension** of an `R`-module `M`, defined as the
171	categorical projective dimension of `ModuleCat.of R M`.
172	
173	This is a re-export of `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` specialised to
174	the abelian category `ModuleCat R`. The categorical definition is the
175	infimum (in `WithBot ℕ∞`) of `n : ℕ` such that all `Ext^i(M, -)` vanish for
176	`i > n`, equivalently the smallest length of a projective resolution of `M`.
177	
178	Mathlib has the categorical `projectiveDimension` and the module-specific
179	`ModuleCat.projectiveDimension_eq_of_linearEquiv`; the blueprint pins the
180	wrapper name so downstream consumers can write
181	`Module.projectiveDimension R M` rather than threading `ModuleCat.of`.
182	
183	The body is the one-line re-export
184	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)`; iter-178 closed
185	this declaration kernel-clean. -/
186	noncomputable def projectiveDimension (R : Type u) [Ring R]
187	    (_M : Type u) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : WithBot ℕ∞ :=
188	  CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)
189	
190	end Module
191	
192	namespace RingTheory
193	
194	namespace Module
