8	/-! # The Jacobian of a smooth proper curve
9	
10	The Jacobian of a smooth, proper, geometrically irreducible curve over a field, equipped with
11	its structure as an abelian variety.
12	
13	## Status (Phase C scaffolding)
14	
15	The Jacobian is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness
16	(`JacobianWitness`) for the curve `C`; the four protected instances on `Jacobian C` all
17	project from the witness directly.
18	
19	This file currently contains TWO `sorry`-bodied declarations (the Phase-C scaffolding
20	inventory):
21	
22	1. `genusZeroWitness` (iter-127 scaffold): a `JacobianWitness C` for a genus-`0` curve,
23	   with underlying scheme `Spec k`. The body is a `sorry` to be closed once pieces
24	   (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile land (iter-138+); the
25	   substantive mathematical content is the genus-`0` rigidity statement
26	   `rigidity_over_kbar` (M2.a; `AlgebraicJacobian.RigidityKbar`).
27	2. `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (Phase-C OFF-LIMITS sorry): existence of an Albanese
28	   witness for an arbitrary smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve; gated on
29	   M2 + M3 closure (Albanese existence in genus `≥ 1` plus genus-`0` rigidity).
30	
31	The file contains:
32	- `IsAlbanese`: the Albanese universal property for a pointed curve.
33	- `IsAlbanese.unique`: uniqueness of the Albanese object up to canonical isomorphism.
34	- `JacobianWitness`: a bundled candidate Albanese object together with the universal
35	  property uniformly over the choice of $k$-rational marked point.
36	- `genusZeroWitness`: an Albanese witness for a genus-`0` curve, body `sorry`
37	  (iter-127 scaffold; closure deferred to iter-138+).
38	- `nonempty_jacobianWitness`: existence of an Albanese witness for every curve —
39	  the headline Phase-C sorry, absorbing both higher-genus Albanese existence and
40	  genus-`0` rigidity.
41	- `Jacobian` and its four protected instances, each obtained by projection from the
42	  Albanese witness.
43	
44	### Forbidden shortcut (sanity check)
45	
46	Defining `Jacobian C := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))` *unconditionally* (the terminal object,
47	i.e. `Spec k`) compiles and discharges three of the four instances for free, but the
48	fourth instance `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (𝟙_ _).hom` would force `genus C = 0`,
49	which is mathematically wrong for any curve of genus `≥ 1`. The terminal-object definition
50	is therefore forbidden when `genus C > 0`. The witness-based definition avoids this
51	issue: in genus `0` the witness's underlying scheme `J` is `Spec k`, and in genus `> 0`
52	the witness's `J` is the honest Albanese object.
53	-/
54	
55	set_option autoImplicit false
56	
57	universe u
58	
59	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
60	
61	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
62	
63	variable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
64	  [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
65	  [IsProper C.hom]
66	  [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
67	
68	/-- The Albanese universal property for a pointed smooth proper curve `(C, P)`.
69	An object `J` (with abelian-variety structure) is the Albanese of `(C, P)` if it
70	receives a universal pointed morphism `C ⟶ J` sending `P` to the identity. -/
71	def IsAlbanese (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) (P : 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k))) ⟶ C)
72	    (J : Over (Spec (.of k)))
73	    [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] : Prop :=
74	  ∃ α : C ⟶ J, P ≫ α = η[J] ∧
75	    ∀ {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom]
76	      [Smooth A.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (_ : P ≫ f = η[A]),
77	      ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = α ≫ g
78	
79	namespace IsAlbanese
80	
81	noncomputable def ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
82	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
83	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) : C ⟶ J :=
84	  Classical.choose h
85	
86	lemma comp_ofCurve {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
87	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
88	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J) :
89	    P ≫ h.ofCurve = η[J] :=
90	  (Classical.choose_spec h).1
91	
92	lemma exists_unique_ofCurve_comp {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C} {J : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj J] [IsProper J.hom] [Smooth J.hom]
94	    [GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom] (h : IsAlbanese C P J)
95	    {A : Over (Spec (.of k))} [GrpObj A] [IsProper A.hom] [Smooth A.hom]
96	    [GeometricallyIrreducible A.hom] (f : C ⟶ A) (hf : P ≫ f = η[A]) :
97	    ∃! (g : J ⟶ A), f = h.ofCurve ≫ g :=
98	  (Classical.choose_spec h).2 f hf
99	
100	/-- The Albanese object is unique up to a unique isomorphism compatible with the
101	universal morphisms. -/
102	theorem unique {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))} {P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C}
103	    {J₁ J₂ : Over (Spec (.of k))}
104	    [GrpObj J₁] [IsProper J₁.hom] [Smooth J₁.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₁.hom]
105	    [GrpObj J₂] [IsProper J₂.hom] [Smooth J₂.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible J₂.hom]
106	    (h₁ : IsAlbanese C P J₁) (h₂ : IsAlbanese C P J₂) :
107	    ∃! (e : J₁ ⟶ J₂), h₂.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ e := by
108	  have ⟨g, hg_eq, hg_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
109	  have ⟨h, hh_eq, hh_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
110	  have ⟨g₁, hg₁_eq, hg₁_unique⟩ := h₁.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₁.ofCurve h₁.comp_ofCurve
111	  have g₁_eq_id : g₁ = 𝟙 J₁ := by
112	    refine (hg₁_unique (𝟙 J₁) ?_).symm
113	    simp
114	  have ggh_eq_g₁ : g ≫ h = g₁ := by
115	    refine hg₁_unique (g ≫ h) ?_
116	    change h₁.ofCurve = h₁.ofCurve ≫ (g ≫ h)
117	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hg_eq, ← hh_eq]
118	  have hgh : g ≫ h = 𝟙 J₁ := by rw [ggh_eq_g₁, g₁_eq_id]
119	  have ⟨k₂, hk₂_eq, hk₂_unique⟩ := h₂.exists_unique_ofCurve_comp h₂.ofCurve h₂.comp_ofCurve
120	  have k₂_eq_id : k₂ = 𝟙 J₂ := by
121	    refine (hk₂_unique (𝟙 J₂) ?_).symm
122	    simp
123	  have hhg_eq_k₂ : h ≫ g = k₂ := by
124	    refine hk₂_unique (h ≫ g) ?_
125	    change h₂.ofCurve = h₂.ofCurve ≫ (h ≫ g)
126	    rw [← Category.assoc, ← hh_eq, ← hg_eq]
127	  have hhg : h ≫ g = 𝟙 J₂ := by rw [hhg_eq_k₂, k₂_eq_id]
128	  exact ⟨g, hg_eq, fun g' hg' => hg_unique g' hg'⟩
129	
130	end IsAlbanese
131	
132	/-- The identity morphism on `Spec k` is geometrically irreducible.
133	This is a small helper needed for the genus-0 case of `Jacobian`. -/
134	lemma geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec (k : Type u) [Field k] :
135	    GeometricallyIrreducible (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))) where
136	  geometrically_irreducibleSpace := by
137	    intro K _ y Z fst snd h
138	    haveI : IsIso snd := h.isIso_snd_of_isIso
139	    exact ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso (P := (IrreducibleSpace · : ObjectProperty Scheme))
140	      (asIso snd).symm inferInstance
141	
142	/-- A bundled Albanese witness for the smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve
143	`C`: a candidate group scheme `J` (with proper, smooth, geometrically irreducible
144	structure, smooth of relative dimension `genus C`), and a proof that `J` satisfies
145	the Albanese universal property of Definition `IsAlbanese` for `C` *for every choice
146	of $k$-rational marked point* `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`.
147	
148	Mathematically, the underlying scheme `J` (and its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
149	structure) is intrinsic to `C`; only the universal morphism `C ⟶ J` depends on the
150	choice of marked point `P`. Encoding `isAlbaneseFor` as a `∀ P, IsAlbanese …` field
151	keeps the bundle independent of any specific pointing, which is what
152	`AbelJacobi.Jacobian.ofCurve P` needs in order to project the Albanese morphism for
153	an *arbitrary* input `P`.
154	
155	The smoothness witness is recorded at the specific relative dimension `genus C`,
156	which is the form required by `Jacobian.smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`. -/
157	structure JacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
158	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] where
159	  /-- The candidate Albanese object. -/
160	  J : Over (Spec (.of k))
161	  /-- The group-scheme structure on `J`. -/
162	  grpObj : GrpObj J
163	  /-- `J` is proper over `k`. -/
164	  proper : IsProper J.hom
165	  /-- `J` is smooth over `k`. -/
166	  smooth : Smooth J.hom
167	  /-- `J` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
168	  geomIrred : GeometricallyIrreducible J.hom
169	  /-- `J` is smooth of relative dimension `genus C` (so dim `J = genus C`). -/
170	  smoothGenus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) J.hom
171	  /-- The Albanese universal-property data: for every choice of $k$-rational marked
172	  point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C`, the scheme `J` together with its group/proper/smooth/irreducible
173	  structure satisfies the Albanese universal property for the pointed curve `(C, P)`. -/
174	  isAlbaneseFor : ∀ (P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C), @IsAlbanese k _ C P J grpObj proper smooth geomIrred
175	
176	/-- Genus-`0` Albanese witness. The witness object is the trivial terminal scheme
177	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec k))`; the `JacobianWitness` structural fields (proper, smooth,
178	geometrically irreducible, genus-stable, group-object) reduce to the
179	corresponding trivialities on `Spec k` via the structural cluster
180	`(𝟙_).hom = 𝟙 (Spec k)`. The `isAlbaneseFor` field consumes the **genus-0
181	abelian-variety rigidity** statement: for every pointed `(C, P)` and every
182	target group scheme `(A, η[A])`, the genus-0 hypothesis forces a morphism
183	`f : C ⟶ A` with `P ≫ f = η[A]` to equal `toUnit C ≫ η[A]`. Over an
184	algebraically closed base `k̄` this is
185	`AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` (in
186	`AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`, char-free, route-C, iter-163).
187	For a general `[Field k]` base the keystone is consumed via pullback to
188	`Spec k̄` + descent of the morphism equality along the faithfully-flat
189	surjection `Spec k̄ → Spec k`. The uniqueness clause cancels the epimorphism
190	`toUnit C` via `Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective` (smooth ⟹ flat,
191	geometrically irreducible ⟹ surjective on `Spec k`).
192	
193	**Status (iter-172):** the `key` body remains `sorry`; the rigidity consumer
194	exists (declared) but still propagates upstream sorries from the iter-171
195	body skeleton in `Genus0BaseObjects.lean`. Wiring closes once
196	(i) the `Genus0BaseObjects` body-skeleton internal sorries discharge, and
197	(ii) the `k → k̄` pullback / descent step is filled. -/
198	noncomputable def genusZeroWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
199	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
200	    (h : genus C = 0) :
201	    JacobianWitness C where
202	  J := 𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of k)))
203	  grpObj := inferInstance
204	  -- `(𝟙_).hom` is defeq `𝟙 (Spec k)`, but instance search does not unfold it;
205	  -- supply each instance for the identity and let term-elaboration close by defeq.
206	  proper := (inferInstance : IsProper (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
207	  smooth := (inferInstance : Smooth (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
208	  geomIrred := geometricallyIrreducible_id_Spec k
209	  smoothGenus := by
210	    rw [h]; exact (inferInstance : SmoothOfRelativeDimension 0 (𝟙 (Spec (.of k))))
211	  isAlbaneseFor := by
212	    -- For every `k`-rational marked point `P`, exhibit the Albanese datum for
213	    -- the pointed curve `(C, P)` with `J = 𝟙_` (blueprint C.2 / C.3).
214	    intro P
215	    -- The universal pointed morphism is the unique terminal map `toUnit C`.
216	    refine ⟨toUnit C, ?_, ?_⟩
217	    · -- Pointed condition: both `P ≫ toUnit C` and `η[𝟙_]` are maps `𝟙_ ⟶ 𝟙_`,
218	      -- equal by uniqueness of morphisms into the terminal object.
219	      exact toUnit_unique _ _
220	    · -- Universal factorisation: given `f : C ⟶ A` with `P ≫ f = η[A]`, the
221	      -- factoring morphism `𝟙_ ⟶ A` is the identity element `η[A]`.
222	      intro A _ _ _ _ f _hf
223	      -- Genus-0 RIGIDITY (C.2): `f` equals the constant morphism at the identity.
224	      -- The route-C content `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` (declared in
225	      -- `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`) is char-free and import-clean
226	      -- (proven iter-163 onward). To consume it for the general `[Field k]` goal,
227	      -- this body needs to pull back `(C, A, f, P)` along an algebraic closure
228	      -- `Spec k̄ → Spec k`, apply `rigidity_genus0_curve_to_grpScheme` in the
229	      -- pulled-back setting, then descend the morphism equality along the
230	      -- faithfully-flat surjection (the descent step itself is
231	      -- `Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective`, already used below to cancel the
232	      -- terminal epimorphism). The pullback functor + the descent of a morphism
233	      -- equality is the residual sub-build; it is real but is not gated on
234	      -- any out-of-file plan-level decision.
235	      have key : f = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by
236	        sorry
237	      refine ⟨η[A], key, ?_⟩
238	      -- Uniqueness: any `g'` with `f = toUnit C ≫ g'` equals `η[A]`, by cancelling
239	      -- the epimorphism `toUnit C`. Its underlying scheme morphism is `C.hom`
240	      -- (`Over.toUnit_left`), which is faithfully flat (smooth ⟹ flat) and
241	      -- surjective (a geometrically irreducible curve over a field is nonempty and
242	      -- `Spec k` is a single point), hence an epimorphism (`Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective`).
243	      intro g' hg'
244	      have hcancel : toUnit C ≫ g' = toUnit C ≫ η[A] := by rw [← hg', ← key]
245	      have hepiL : Epi (toUnit C).left := by
246	        rw [Over.toUnit_left]; exact Flat.epi_of_flat_of_surjective C.hom
247	      haveI : Epi (toUnit C) := Over.epi_of_epi_left _
248	      exact (cancel_epi (toUnit C)).mp hcancel
249	
250	/-- The Albanese witness for a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C`
251	of positive genus `g ≥ 1` over `k`. The underlying scheme is the Albanese
252	variety of `C` — classically the connected component of the identity of
253	`Pic_{C/k}`, or equivalently the Stein factorisation of the Abel–Jacobi
254	morphism on `Sym^g(C)`. Both constructions require infrastructure not yet
255	in Mathlib (FGA representability of the Picard functor for Route A;
256	symmetric powers + finite-group quotients + Stein factorisation +
257	Brill–Noether–Riemann–Roch for Route B); the iter-123 M3 route audit
258	(`analogies/m3-route-audit.md`) records both at midpoint ~6500 and
259	~9000 LOC respectively.
260	
261	**Status**: iter-134 scaffold — body is `sorry`. The body closure is M3
262	work, currently OFF-CRITICAL-PATH until M2 closes (per STRATEGY.md
263	§ M3, user-escalation-pending).
264	
265	**Iter-135**: the genus-stratified body restructure of
266	`nonempty_jacobianWitness` is now in place (Change 2.1 of iter-135
267	refactor); this scaffold is the positive-genus arm. Body closure
268	remains M3 work (off-critical-path; user-escalation-pending per
269	`analogies/m3-route-audit.md`). -/
270	noncomputable def positiveGenusWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
271	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
272	    (hg : 0 < genus C) :
273	    JacobianWitness C :=
274	  sorry
275	
276	/-- Existence of an Albanese witness for every smooth proper geometrically irreducible
277	curve. This is one of the two open mathematical sorries of the Phase-C Jacobian
278	scaffolding (the other being `genusZeroWitness`); both are scheduled for body
279	closure post M2 + M3 per STRATEGY.md. It packages all five protected sorries
280	(`Jacobian`, `instGrpObj`, `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus`, `instIsProper`,
281	`instGeometricallyIrreducible`) into a single existence hypothesis, *and* records
282	that the Albanese property of the underlying scheme `J` is uniform over every
283	choice of $k$-rational marked point `P : 𝟙_ _ ⟶ C` (via the `isAlbaneseFor`
284	field of `JacobianWitness`).
285	
286	Mathematically this corresponds to the construction of the Albanese variety of `C`,
287	classically via symmetric powers $\mathrm{Sym}^g(C)$ and the Abel--Jacobi map
288	(Brill--Noether--Riemann--Roch), or equivalently as the identity component of the
289	Picard scheme. Both routes require infrastructure not yet available in Mathlib
290	(quotients of schemes by finite group actions; FGA representability), so the
291	existence is assumed.
292	
293	**Iter-135 body restructure**: the body is now a `by_cases h :
294	genus C = 0` decomposition consuming `genusZeroWitness` (genus-0 arm)
295	and `positiveGenusWitness` (positive-genus arm). Both arms' bodies
296	remain `sorry` (M2.b body closure iter-138+; M3 body closure
297	off-critical-path); the restructure converts the inline `sorry` here
298	into honest delegation to the two scaffolds, removing one inline-`sorry`
299	site without prejudging either arm's body closure. -/
300	theorem nonempty_jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
301	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
302	    Nonempty (JacobianWitness C) := by
303	  by_cases h : genus C = 0
304	  · exact ⟨genusZeroWitness C h⟩
305	  · exact ⟨positiveGenusWitness C (Nat.pos_of_ne_zero h)⟩
306	
307	/-- A choice of Albanese witness for `C`, extracted via `Classical.choice`.
308	Used to define `Jacobian C` and to discharge each of the four protected instances
309	on it. -/
310	noncomputable def jacobianWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
311	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] :
312	    JacobianWitness C :=
313	  Classical.choice (nonempty_jacobianWitness C)
314	
315	-- data
316	/-- The Jacobian of a smooth, proper curve over a field `k`.
317	
318	The Jacobian is the underlying scheme of an Albanese witness for `C` (see
319	`JacobianWitness` and `nonempty_jacobianWitness`); the existence of such a
320	witness is one of the two open mathematical sorries of the Phase-C scaffolding
321	(the other being `genusZeroWitness`), both scheduled for body closure post
322	M2 + M3 per STRATEGY.md. The genus-`0` specialisation is implicit in the
323	witness — a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme over `k` of
324	relative dimension `0` is `Spec k` — and the genus-`0` rigidity content is
325	absorbed into `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. -/
326	noncomputable def Jacobian (C : Over (Spec (.of k))) [IsProper C.hom] [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom]
327	    [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom] : Over (Spec (.of k)) :=
328	  (jacobianWitness C).J
329	
330	namespace Jacobian
331	
332	/-! ## The Jacobian of `C` is an abelian variety. -/
333	
334	-- data
335	/-- The group scheme structure on the Jacobian of the curve `C`. -/
336	noncomputable instance instGrpObj : GrpObj (Jacobian C) := (jacobianWitness C).grpObj
337	
338	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is smooth of relative dimension `g` over `k`, where `g` is the
339	genus of `C`. -/
340	instance smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus : SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C) (Jacobian C).hom :=
341	  (jacobianWitness C).smoothGenus
342	
343	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is proper over `k`. -/
344	instance instIsProper : IsProper (Jacobian C).hom := (jacobianWitness C).proper
345	
346	/-- The Jacobian of `C` is geometrically irreducible over `k`. -/
347	instance instGeometricallyIrreducible : GeometricallyIrreducible (Jacobian C).hom :=
348	  (jacobianWitness C).geomIrred
349	
350	end Jacobian
351	
352	end AlgebraicGeometry

207	`P₀ ∈ C(k̄)`. On `k̄`-points,
208	`ι_{P₀}(Q) = [𝓞_C(Q − P₀)] ∈ Pic⁰_{C/k̄}(k̄)`; at the basepoint

211	This is the content gated on A.3 (existence of `Pic⁰_{C/k̄}` as a

213	the rigidified diagonal correspondence
214	`𝓛^{P₀} = 𝓞_{C × C}(Δ − {P₀} × C − C × {P₀})` on `C × C`, taken as a
215	relative degree-zero line bundle on `C × C` over the second factor.
216	
217	Blueprint reference: `lem:abel_jacobi_morphism` (Milne, *Abelian
218	Varieties*, Proposition III.6.1 and Summary III.6.11). -/

151	/-! ## §1. Pin 1 — the morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹` from a globally-generating pair
152	(`morphismToP1OfGlobalSections`)
153	
154	Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, specialised to `ℙ¹` over `k̄`. Given a graded
155	`k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` whose image of the
156	irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)` generates the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, the
157	construction `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of Mathlib's
158	`AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic` produces a morphism
159	`X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme k̄`. Lifting to the slice
160	category over `Spec k̄` (via `Over.homMk` with a section-condition
161	proof) yields the pinned morphism
162	`X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄ : Over (Spec (.of kbar))`. -/
163	
164	/-- **The morphism `X ⟶ ℙ¹_{k̄}` produced from a globally-generating pair of
165	homogeneous sections** (Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1; Mathlib's
166	`AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` specialised to the standard
167	ℕ-grading on `k̄[X₀, X₁]`).
168	
169	Given a graded `k̄`-algebra homomorphism `f : k̄[X₀, X₁] →+* Γ(X, ⊤)`, a
170	`k̄`-algebra compatibility witness `halg` (recording that the composite
171	`kbar → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` agrees with the natural
172	algebra map `kbar → Γ(X.left, ⊤)` induced by `X.hom`), and a proof `hf`
173	that the image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁) ⊆ k̄[X₀, X₁]` generates
174	the unit ideal of `Γ(X, ⊤)`, this constructs the unique morphism
175	`φ : X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar k̄` characterised on basic-opens by
176	`φ ⁻¹ᵁ D₊(X_i) = X.basicOpen (f X_i)` (the "no-common-zero" expression of
177	Hartshorne's morphism condition). The composite
178	`φ.left ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ = X.hom`, witnessing the `Over (Spec k̄)`
179	section condition.
180	
181	The input data `(f, halg, hf)` is the standard Hartshorne II §7 packaging
182	of "a pair of global sections `s₀, s₁ ∈ H⁰(X, ℒ)` of an invertible sheaf
183	`ℒ` without common zeros": `f` is the graded `k̄`-algebra map sending
184	`X_i ↦ s_i ∈ Γ(X, ℒ) ⊂ Γ(X, ⊤)` (after a choice of trivialisation
185	identifying `H⁰(X, ℒ)` with a submodule of `Γ(X, ⊤)` over the affine
186	cover `X = D(s₀) ∪ D(s₁)`); `halg` is the `kbar`-algebra-compatibility
187	condition (without which the section condition over `Spec kbar` cannot
188	be derived); `hf` is the no-common-zero condition.
189	
190	The construction invokes `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` on the standard
191	grading `projectiveLineBarGrading k̄` (the `ℕ`-grading on
192	`MvPolynomial (Fin 2) k̄` by total degree, with `GradedRing` instance
193	via `MvPolynomial.gradedAlgebra`), then wraps in `Over.homMk` with the
194	section condition proof. The section condition chases through
195	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` plus
196	`IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)` to reduce to
197	`X.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (f.comp MvPolynomial.C)) = X.hom`,
198	which the `halg` hypothesis closes via `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality` and
199	`toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv` (cf. the analogous chain in
200	`Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean:pointOfVec`).
201	
202	Blueprint reference: `lem:morphism_to_p1_from_global_sections`
203	(Hartshorne II Theorem 7.1, II §7). -/
204	noncomputable def morphismToP1OfGlobalSections
205	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar]
206	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
207	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(X.left, ⊤))
208	    (_halg :
209	      f.comp (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) =
210	        X.hom.appTop.hom.comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom)
211	    (_hf :
212	      Ideal.map f
213	          (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤) :
214	    X ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
215	  -- Underlying scheme morphism: invoke Mathlib's
216	  -- `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` on `projectiveLineBarGrading kbar`.
217	  -- The result `X.left ⟶ Proj 𝒜 = ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar` is
218	  -- then wrapped via `Over.homMk` with the section condition
219	  -- `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections … ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = X.hom`.
220	  Over.homMk
221	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f _hf) <| by
222	    -- Section condition (`Over (Spec k̄)`-compatibility):
223	    --   `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections … ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = X.hom`
224	    -- where `ProjectiveLineBar.hom = Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map (algebraMap kbar ↥(𝒜 0))`.
225	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
226	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
227	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq
228	        (R := kbar) (S := ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0))
229	        (A := MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (fun _ => rfl)

9	/-!
10	# Weil divisors on a smooth proper curve (RR.1)
11	
12	This file is the **RR.1** sub-build chapter for the project's headline
13	`genusZero_curve_iso_P1` (the "smooth proper geom-irred genus-`0` curve over `k̄` is
14	isomorphic to `ℙ¹`" lemma in `AlgebraicJacobian.AbelianVarietyRigidity`).
15	
16	Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `WeilDivisor` on a scheme; adjacent pieces
17	(`MeromorphicOn.divisor`, `CommRing.Pic`, `Scheme.RationalMap`) cover different
18	ground. This file is therefore **project-bespoke**, scaffolding the formal-sum data
19	type `Div(X) = ⨁_{Y ⊂ X codim 1} ℤ` on a Noetherian integral scheme `X` satisfying
20	Hartshorne's condition `(*)`, the principal-divisor homomorphism
21	`div : K(X)^× → Div(X)` on a curve, the degree map `deg : Div(C) → ℤ` on a smooth
22	proper curve over an algebraically closed field, the degree-zero of principal divisors
23	on a complete nonsingular curve (Hartshorne Cor. II.6.10), and the linear-equivalence
24	relation `D ~ D'`.
25	
26	## Status (iter-172 file-skeleton)
27	
28	This file is the **iter-172 Lane C** file-skeleton: each declaration carries the
29	intended signature (matching the blueprint `\lean{...}` pin) with a `sorry` body.
30	The bodies are iter-173+ work after the sibling chapters `RR.2`
31	(`RiemannRoch_RRFormula.tex`), `RR.3` (`RiemannRoch_OcOfD.tex`), and `RR.4`
32	(`RiemannRoch_RationalIsoP1.tex`) land.
33	
34	The 9 pinned declarations are:
35	
36	1. `Scheme.WeilDivisor` — free abelian group on prime divisors (Definition).
37	2. `Scheme.RationalMap.order` — order of a rational function along a prime divisor.
38	3. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.ofClosedPoint` — Weil divisor associated to a closed point.
39	4. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree` — degree map over an algebraically closed base.
40	5. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.degree_hom` — degree is a group homomorphism (Theorem).
41	6. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal` — principal divisor of a rational function.
42	7. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_hom` — `div` is a group homomorphism (Theorem).
43	8. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.principal_degree_zero` — `deg ∘ div = 0` on a complete curve.
44	9. `Scheme.WeilDivisor.LinearEquivalence` — linear equivalence of Weil divisors.

62	/-! ## §1. Codim-1 cycle group / Weil divisor group
63	
64	Hartshorne's condition `(*)` (II §6, p. 130) is: `X` is a Noetherian integral
65	separated scheme that is regular in codimension one. Under `(*)`, prime divisors
66	are closed integral subschemes of codimension one, and the local ring at the
67	generic point of a prime divisor is a DVR with quotient field the function field
68	`K(X)`.
69	
70	A prime divisor is encoded by its **generic point** together with the
71	codimension-one witness `Order.coheight point = 1`: the data field `point : X`
72	selects the generic point of the closed integral subscheme, and the predicate
73	field `coheight` enforces codimension one in the specialisation preorder
74	(Hartshorne II §6 p. 130; blueprint pin `def:prime_divisor`). Integrality of the
75	closure is automatic, so no separate integrality witness is needed.
76	-/
77	
78	/-- A **prime divisor** on a scheme `X`: a closed integral subscheme of codimension
79	one, encoded (for a scheme satisfying Hartshorne's condition `(*)`) by its generic
80	point. On a curve, prime divisors correspond bijectively to closed points.
81	
82	The codimension-one witness is the predicate `Order.coheight point = 1` on the
83	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier` (Mathlib's convention is
84	`x ≤ y ↔ y ⤳ x`, so the generic point is the unique maximal element and a
85	generic point of a codim-1 closed integral subscheme has a length-one chain
86	above it). The closure `{point}̄` is automatically irreducible (so the reduced
87	induced subscheme structure is automatically integral); hence no separate
88	integrality field is needed.
89	
90	Blueprint reference: `def:prime_divisor` / `def:codim1_cycles` (Hartshorne II §6
91	p. 130; Stacks 02RW). -/
92	structure Scheme.PrimeDivisor (X : Scheme.{u}) where
93	  /-- The generic point of the closed integral subscheme. -/
94	  point : X
95	  /-- Codimension-1 witness: `point` has coheight `1` in the specialisation
96	  preorder on `X.carrier`. Per iter-173 `wd-spec-refine` (`def:prime_divisor`). -/
97	  coheight : Order.coheight point = 1
98	
99	/-- The **Weil divisor group** of a scheme `X` satisfying Hartshorne's condition
100	`(*)`: the free abelian group on the set of prime divisors of `X`,

100	Blueprint reference: `def:line_bundle_on_product` (Kleiman §2, "absolute Picard
101	functor"; Stacks tag 01HK). -/
102	
103	/-- The type of **line bundles on the product** `C ×_S T`: invertible sheaves of
104	`O_(C ×_S T)`-modules, where the product is the categorical pullback of the
105	projections `πC : C ⟶ S` and `πT : T ⟶ S`. In Kleiman's notation with `X = C`
106	and base `S`, this is `Pic_C(T) = Pic(C ×_S T)`, the value of the absolute
107	Picard functor at `T`.
108	
109	iter-175+: instantiate as a subtype of `(pullback πC πT).Modules` carrying an
110	invertibility witness — concretely either
111	```
112	structure OnProduct ... where
113	  sheaf : (pullback πC πT).Modules
114	  isInvertible : IsInvertible sheaf
115	```
116	or the quotient by isomorphism for the Picard *group* refinement. For the
117	iter-174 file-skeleton this is a typed `sorry` at the type level because
118	Mathlib `b80f227` ships no `IsInvertible` predicate on `Scheme.Modules`. -/
119	noncomputable def OnProduct {S C T : Scheme.{u}} (_πC : C ⟶ S) (_πT : T ⟶ S) :
120	    Type (u+1) :=
121	  sorry
122	
123	/-! ## §2. The pullback functor along the projection
124	
125	For a morphism of schemes `f : Y ⟶ Z` and an invertible `O_Z`-module `M`, the
126	pullback `f^* M` is an invertible `O_Y`-module (the pullback of an
127	open-cover-trivialisation of `M` trivialises `f^* M`, and the pullback of a free
128	module of rank one is free of rank one — Stacks tag 01HH). Specialising to the
129	projection `π_T = pullback.snd πC πT : C ×_S T ⟶ T` gives the pullback map
130	`π_T^* : Pic(T) → Pic(C ×_S T)`. Mathlib's underlying `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
131	functor (in `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Modules.Sheaf`) supplies the pullback on
132	the full category of sheaves of modules; iter-175+ will restrict it to
133	invertible sheaves once the `OnProduct` carrier is unpacked.
134	
135	Blueprint reference: `def:pullback_along_projection` (Kleiman §2; Stacks tag
136	01HH). -/
137	
138	/-- **Pullback along the projection** `π_T : C ×_S T ⟶ T` of a relative
139	curve. Sends an `O_T`-line bundle `N` to the `O_(C ×_S T)`-line bundle
140	`π_T^* N`, induced by the underlying `Scheme.Modules.pullback` functor on
141	`Scheme.Modules`.
142	
143	iter-175+: instantiate as `(Scheme.Modules.pullback (pullback.snd πC πT)).obj N`
144	wrapped with the carried `IsInvertible` witness (the pullback of an invertible
145	sheaf is invertible, Stacks 01HH). For the iter-174 file-skeleton the body is
146	a typed `sorry` because the carrier type `OnProduct` is itself a typed `sorry`
147	(see §1). The signature is non-tautological: it asserts a *function*
148	`T.Modules → OnProduct πC πT`, which after unfolding `OnProduct` becomes the
149	concrete pullback functor restricted to invertible modules. -/

  body lands. The Lean as Lean is sound and the proof composition is correct — only
  the iter-180 audit-of-self claim is inflated.
